---
title: "Examples"
toc: false
---

Worked examples ported from the USACE-RMC
[Numerics-Python-Examples](https://github.com/USACE-RMC/Numerics-Python-Examples)
repository (0BSD licensed). Each example exists in a Python version (`corehydropy`,
a Jupyter notebook you can download and run) and an R version (`corehydror`, a
Quarto document). The pair uses the same seeds and prints the same numbers: both
packages run the same compiled core with a bit-exact Mersenne Twister.

Every example ends with a reproduction check comparing this port's results
against the outputs embedded in the upstream notebooks, run by the original C#
library. Where a result is seeded and deterministic the match is exact; where an
example was recast onto this package's API the check is statistical and says so.
The [coverage page](coverage.qmd) lists every upstream example and its status
here.

| # | Example | Python | R |
|---|---------|--------|---|
| 00 | Getting started | [Python](00-getting-started/python.ipynb) | [R](00-getting-started/r.qmd) |
| 01 | Probability distributions | [Python](01-distributions/python.ipynb) | [R](01-distributions/r.qmd) |
| 02 | Distribution fitting | [Python](02-distribution-fitting/python.ipynb) | [R](02-distribution-fitting/r.qmd) |
| 03 | MCMC basics | [Python](03-mcmc-basics/python.ipynb) | [R](03-mcmc-basics/r.qmd) |
| 04 | Bayesian inference | [Python](04-bayesian-inference/python.ipynb) | [R](04-bayesian-inference/r.qmd) |
| 05 | Adaptive MCMC samplers | [Python](05-mcmc-samplers/python.ipynb) | [R](05-mcmc-samplers/r.qmd) |
| 06 | MCMC diagnostics | [Python](06-mcmc-diagnostics/python.ipynb) | [R](06-mcmc-diagnostics/r.qmd) |
| 09 | Statistics toolbox | [Python](09-statistics/python.ipynb) | [R](09-statistics/r.qmd) |
| 20 | Bayesian regression | [Python](20-bayesian-regression/python.ipynb) | [R](20-bayesian-regression/r.qmd) |
| 21 | Flood frequency analysis | [Python](21-flood-frequency/python.ipynb) | [R](21-flood-frequency/r.qmd) |
| 22 | Reliability analysis | [Python](22-reliability/python.ipynb) | [R](22-reliability/r.qmd) |

Numbers 00-09 mirror the upstream notebooks; 20-22 are the upstream's three
standalone scripts.
